# Work Packages: Stage Profile Enrichment

**Inputs**: Design documents from `kitty-specs/002-stage-profile-enrichment/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, contracts/api.md, quickstart.md

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package must be independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `kitty-specs/002-stage-profile-enrichment/tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`.

---

## Work Package WP01: Schema, Shared Types & Domain Entity (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Add the ParcoursType enum and 4 new nullable columns to the `race_results` table, update shared types, and extend the domain entity.
**Independent Test**: `pnpm build` succeeds, Drizzle migration generates cleanly, shared types compile across all packages.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP01-schema-and-shared-types.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~350 lines

**Requirements Refs**: FR-005, FR-010

### Included Subtasks

- [ ] T001 [P] Add `ParcoursType` enum to `packages/shared-types/src/enums.ts`
- [ ] T002 Add `parcoursTypeEnum` to `apps/api/src/infrastructure/database/schema/enums.ts`
- [ ] T003 Add 4 columns (`parcours_type`, `is_itt`, `is_ttt`, `profile_score`) to `apps/api/src/infrastructure/database/schema/race-results.ts`
- [ ] T004 Generate Drizzle migration for the schema changes
- [ ] T005 Update `RaceResultProps` and `RaceResult` entity in `apps/api/src/domain/race-result/race-result.entity.ts`
- [ ] T006 [P] Update `ParsedResult` interface in `apps/api/src/infrastructure/scraping/parsers/parsed-result.type.ts`

### Implementation Notes

- New enum values: `p1`, `p2`, `p3`, `p4`, `p5` вҖ” matching PCS CSS class names.
- All 4 new columns are nullable to support existing rows where profile doesn't apply (GC/MOUNTAIN/SPRINT in stage races).
- `is_itt` and `is_ttt` default to `false`.
- Domain entity getters follow existing pattern (readonly props, getter accessors).

### Parallel Opportunities

- T001 (shared types) and T006 (parsed result type) can be done in parallel with T002-T005.

### Dependencies

- None (foundation package).

### Risks & Mitigations

- Migration must be compatible with existing data вҶ’ since DB will be re-seeded, this is low risk.

---

## Work Package WP02: Profile Extractor & Parser Updates (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Create a shared profile extractor utility and integrate it into the existing stage-race and classic parsers to capture parcours type, ProfileScore, and ITT/TTT flags during scraping.
**Independent Test**: Parser tests pass with profile data assertions. Scraping a known race produces results with profile fields populated.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP02-profile-extractor-and-parsers.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~500 lines

**Requirements Refs**: FR-001, FR-002, FR-003, FR-004, FR-010

### Included Subtasks

- [ ] T007 Create `apps/api/src/infrastructure/scraping/parsers/profile-extractor.ts` with `extractParcoursType()`, `extractProfileScore()`, and `detectTimeTrialType()` functions
- [ ] T008 Modify `apps/api/src/infrastructure/scraping/parsers/stage-race.parser.ts` вҖ” `parseStageResults()` must accept stage HTML and extract profile via profile-extractor
- [ ] T009 Modify `apps/api/src/infrastructure/scraping/parsers/classic.parser.ts` вҖ” `parseClassicResults()` must extract profile from sidebar
- [ ] T010 Update `apps/api/src/application/scraping/trigger-scrape.use-case.ts` to pass profile data through from parsers to `RaceResult.create()`
- [ ] T011 Create HTML fixture files for profile extraction tests (stage result sidebar, classic result sidebar)
- [ ] T012 Write unit tests for profile-extractor and updated parsers

### Implementation Notes

- `extractParcoursType(html)`: Use Cheerio to find `span.icon.profile`, extract `p1`-`p5` from class list via regex `/\bp(\d)\b/`.
- `extractProfileScore(html)`: Find `.infolist li` with title "ProfileScore", parse integer value.
- `detectTimeTrialType(stageNameText)`: Regex `/\(ITT\)/i` and `/\(TTT\)/i` on stage link text.
- The stage-race parser currently calls `parseStageResults(html, stageNumber)` which delegates to `parseResultsTable()`. The profile data needs to be extracted from the same HTML (individual stage page) and attached to each ParsedResult.
- The classic parser wraps `parseResultsTable()` вҖ” profile needs extracting from the same result page HTML.
- `trigger-scrape.use-case.ts` maps `ParsedResult` to `RaceResult.create()` вҖ” the new profile fields must be passed through.

### Parallel Opportunities

- T011 (fixtures) can be prepared while T007-T010 are being implemented.

### Dependencies

- Depends on WP01 (needs ParcoursType enum, updated ParsedResult interface, updated RaceResult entity).

### Risks & Mitigations

- PCS sidebar HTML may vary between pages вҶ’ null fallback on extraction failure.
- Existing fixtures may not contain profile sidebar data вҶ’ new fixtures needed with profile HTML.

---

## Work Package WP03: Race Overview Parser (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Create a new parser that extracts the stage list with profile distribution from a PCS race overview page.
**Independent Test**: Given HTML of a race overview page, parser returns correct stage list with parcours types, ITT/TTT flags, distances, and cities.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP03-race-overview-parser.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~350 lines

**Requirements Refs**: FR-006, FR-009

### Included Subtasks

- [ ] T013 Create `apps/api/src/infrastructure/scraping/parsers/race-overview.parser.ts` with `parseRaceOverview()` function
- [ ] T014 Handle edge cases: rest days (skip), TBD stages (null parcours), prologues, ITT/TTT detection from stage name
- [ ] T015 Create HTML fixture for a stage race overview page (based on TdF 2024)
- [ ] T016 Write unit tests for `parseRaceOverview()`

### Implementation Notes

- Parse the `table.basic` under `<h4>Stages</h4>` heading.
- Each row has 5 columns: date (DD/MM), day, profile icon (`span.icon.profile.p{N}`), stage link (with name + departure/arrival), distance (km).
- Rest days: profile class is `p` (no digit) AND link text is "Restday" вҶ’ skip.
- ITT/TTT: detected from stage link text via regex, same as profile-extractor.
- Return type: `ParsedStageInfo[]` with `stageNumber`, `parcoursType`, `isItt`, `isTtt`, `distanceKm`, `departure`, `arrival`.

### Parallel Opportunities

- This WP can run in parallel with WP02 (independent parser, no shared code dependency).

### Dependencies

- Depends on WP01 (needs ParcoursType enum).

### Risks & Mitigations

- Overview page structure may differ for very small races вҶ’ parser should gracefully return empty array if no stage table found.

---

## Work Package WP04: Race Profile Use Case & API Endpoint (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Create the `FetchRaceProfile` use case and `GET /api/race-profile` endpoint that fetches and returns a race's profile distribution from its PCS URL.
**Independent Test**: Call `GET /api/race-profile?url=https://www.procyclingstats.com/race/tour-de-france/2024` and receive correct profile distribution.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP04-race-profile-endpoint.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~450 lines

**Requirements Refs**: FR-006, FR-007, FR-007b, FR-008, FR-009

### Included Subtasks

- [ ] T017 [P] Add `RaceProfileResponse`, `StageInfo`, `ProfileSummary` types to `packages/shared-types/src/api.ts`
- [ ] T018 Create `apps/api/src/application/analyze/fetch-race-profile.use-case.ts` вҖ” orchestrate profile fetch from PCS URL
- [ ] T019 Implement race type detection (classic vs stage race) and classic fallback to previous year
- [ ] T020 Create `apps/api/src/presentation/race-profile.controller.ts` вҖ” `GET /api/race-profile`
- [ ] T021 Register controller and use case in NestJS module
- [ ] T022 Write tests for use case (mock PCS client) and controller

### Implementation Notes

- URL parsing: extract `raceSlug` and `year` from PCS URL pattern `/race/{slug}/{year}`.
- For stage races: fetch overview page вҶ’ use race-overview.parser вҶ’ build profile summary.
- For classics: fetch result page вҶ’ use profile-extractor вҶ’ if 404, try `year-1` (fallback).
- Race type auto-detection: if overview page has stage table вҶ’ stage race; if slug matches known GT list вҶ’ grand_tour; else вҶ’ mini_tour. If no stage table вҶ’ classic.
- Profile summary: count p1-p5 occurrences, ITT/TTT counts, unknown count.

### Parallel Opportunities

- T017 (shared types) can be done in parallel with T018-T020.

### Dependencies

- Depends on WP02 (profile-extractor) and WP03 (race-overview.parser).

### Risks & Mitigations

- PCS may rate-limit during profile fetch вҶ’ existing throttling in PCS client handles this.
- Future classics without result page вҶ’ fallback logic to previous year.

---

## Work Package WP05: Frontend вҖ” URL Input & Profile Display (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Replace the manual race type selector with a PCS URL input field, fetch the race profile distribution, and display it in the analysis form.
**Independent Test**: Paste a PCS race URL вҶ’ see profile distribution displayed вҶ’ race type auto-detected вҶ’ submit rider list works with auto-detected race type.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP05-frontend-url-input.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~400 lines

**Requirements Refs**: FR-008, FR-009

### Included Subtasks

- [ ] T023 Create `apps/web/src/features/rider-list/components/race-profile-summary.tsx` component
- [ ] T024 Modify `apps/web/src/features/rider-list/components/rider-input.tsx` вҖ” replace race type selector with PCS URL input
- [ ] T025 Add API hook to call `GET /api/race-profile` on URL input (debounced)
- [ ] T026 Auto-fill race type from profile response, handle loading/error/empty states
- [ ] T027 Update `AnalyzeRequest` submission to use auto-detected race type

### Implementation Notes

- URL input: text field with placeholder "Paste PCS race URL (e.g., https://www.procyclingstats.com/race/tour-de-france/2025)".
- On URL paste/change (debounced ~500ms): call `/api/race-profile?url=...`.
- Loading state: show spinner/skeleton while fetching profile.
- Error state: show inline error message if URL invalid or PCS unreachable.
- Profile summary display: visual summary showing count per parcours type (use icons or colored badges for p1-p5), ITT/TTT count, total stages.
- Race type auto-detection: show detected type (e.g., "Grand Tour") as a read-only badge.
- The existing race type dropdown is removed entirely.
- Use shadcn/ui components and Tailwind CSS consistent with existing design.

### Parallel Opportunities

- T023 (component) and T025 (API hook) can be built in parallel.

### Dependencies

- Depends on WP04 (needs `/api/race-profile` endpoint).

### Risks & Mitigations

- Slow profile fetch (up to 30s) вҶ’ clear loading indicator, consider caching recent results client-side.

---

## Work Package WP06: Database Re-seed & Validation (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Wipe the database and re-seed from scratch with the enriched schema, then validate that profile data is correctly populated.
**Independent Test**: After re-seed, query stage results and verify parcours_type, is_itt, is_ttt, and profile_score are populated correctly for at least 3 known races (SC-005).
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP06-reseed-and-validation.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~300 lines

**Requirements Refs**: FR-011

### Included Subtasks

- [ ] T028 Verify `seed-database` CLI command works with the updated schema and parsers
- [ ] T029 Run full re-seed for at least 2 seasons of data
- [ ] T030 Validate profile data for 3 known races: TdF 2024 (stage race), Paris-Nice 2024 (mini tour), Milano-Sanremo 2024 (classic)
- [ ] T031 Verify existing scoring pipeline and `/api/analyze` still work correctly with enriched data

### Implementation Notes

- The `seed-database` command already handles race discovery and scraping orchestration. The updated parsers (WP02) will now produce profile data automatically.
- Validation queries: check that stage results have non-null `parcours_type`, GC/mountain/sprint rows have null `parcours_type`.
- For TdF 2024: verify stage 1 is p4, stage 7 (ITT) has `is_itt=true` and p1, stage 11 is p5.
- For MSR 2024: verify GC row has parcours_type=p2.
- Run existing `/api/analyze` endpoint to confirm no regression in scoring.

### Parallel Opportunities

- None вҖ” sequential validation after re-seed.

### Dependencies

- Depends on WP02 (updated parsers produce profile data).

### Risks & Mitigations

- Re-seed takes time (polite scraping delays) вҶ’ expected, not a concern.
- Some races may fail to provide ProfileScore вҶ’ accepted as null (FR-010).

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

- **Sequence**: WP01 вҶ’ (WP02 вҖ– WP03) вҶ’ WP04 вҶ’ WP05
- **Parallel group 1**: WP02 and WP03 can run in parallel after WP01
- **Parallel group 2**: WP05 and WP06 can run in parallel after WP04 and WP02 respectively
- **MVP Scope**: WP01 + WP02 + WP06 = profile data captured during scraping (US0 complete)
- **Full scope**: All 6 WPs = US0 + US1 + US2 complete

```
WP01 в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ WP02 в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ WP04 в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ WP05
     в””в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ WP03 в”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ WP06 (after WP02)
```
